questions.question: What does Mandarin contrast in its stop consonants (b/p, d/t, g/k)?
options: Aspiration (a puff of air on release)
questions.explanation: The b/p, d/t, g/k contrast is aspiration, not voicing. Pinyin 'b' is closer to English unvoiced p than to English voiced b.

questions.question: How is the syllable 'ju' actually pronounced?
options: Like English 'joo' with a regular u
Like 'jü' — the u is really ü, but ü is spelled u after j/q/x/y
questions.explanation: After j, q, x, and y, the letter u is always pronounced ü. The umlaut is dropped in spelling, not in pronunciation.

questions.question: When two tone-3 syllables come in a row, what happens?
questions.explanation: Tone-3 sandhi: the first of two tone-3 syllables shifts to tone 2. 你好 is written nǐ hǎo but pronounced ní hǎo.

questions.question: How is 不 (bù) pronounced in 不是?
questions.explanation: 不 shifts from tone 4 to tone 2 when followed by another tone-4 syllable. 是 is tone 4, so 不是 is pronounced bú shì.

questions.question: Which pinyin row is the retroflex group (tongue curled back)?
questions.explanation: zh, ch, sh, and r are retroflex — tongue tip curled up toward the hard palate. j/q/x are palatal; z/c/s are alveolar.

questions.question: Which component means 'water' and appears as 氵 on the left side of characters?
questions.explanation: 水 (shuǐ, water) appears as the radical 氵 (three drops) when placed on the left side of a character: 河, 湖, 海, 江.

questions.question: Which is true about Mandarin syllable structure?
options: Syllables may end in any consonant
Syllables can end in vowels, -n, -ng, or the -r suffix; no final stops
Every syllable must end with a vowel
Consonant clusters of two or three consonants are allowed at the end
questions.explanation: Mandarin has no final stops and no consonant clusters. A syllable ends in a vowel, -n, -ng, or the retroflex -r suffix.

questions.question: What is the correct description of tone 3 inside a sentence (not in isolation)?
options: Almost always pronounced as a low tone (half-third)
questions.explanation: In connected speech, tone 3 is usually pronounced as a sustained low tone (half-third). Singing the full dip is a beginner tell.

questions.question: Which component originally depicted a person from the side and appears inside 你, 他, 们?
questions.explanation: 人 (rén, person) is a pictograph of a walking person. It appears as 亻 on the left of characters like 你, 他, 们, 休.

questions.question: Why is the neutral tone called 'no tone' rather than 'tone 0'?
options: Its pitch is unstressed and inherits from the preceding syllable; the duration is short
It only appears in foreign loanwords
questions.explanation: Neutral-tone syllables are short and unstressed; their pitch follows the previous tone. The duration cue is as important as the pitch.
